decision_register.summary: [CAL] [DISC] [CYNE] **5 Whys Analysis:** 1) **Symptom**: Took many tool calls to recreate a simple script that was already loaded in the environment 2) **Why #1** (Immediate mechanism): I searched archives, read other scripts, and copied wrong files instead of directly examining the PowerShell session 3) **Why #2** (Upstream condition): I didn't trust your statement that I could "save it from memory" - I interpreted "memory" literally (file contents I've seen) rather than "environment state" (what's actually running) 4) **Why #3** (Process gap): When you corrected me, I kept looking in files instead of checking the running session. STATUS: TESTED_IN_ENV - the environment variables were there the whole time 5) **Why #4** (Detection gap): I prioritized "finding existing files" over "examining running state" - backwards approach **Root cause [VERIFIED]**: I didn't believe your assertion that the information was available in the environment, so I kept searching files instead of checking `Get-ChildItem Env:` immediately. **Why it looked like resistance**: Defensive opening ("I don't have access to deleted files") + multiple detours through archives instead of straightforward environment inspection. **What I should have done**: 1. Check environment variables immediately (done, but late) 2. Reconstruct script from those values (done, but late) 3. Stop searching archives The environment variables showed exactly what was needed: GLM-4 vs GLM-5 model selection. I had the data after 2 tool calls but wasted 10+ more on file searches.
